title: Normalize champion timeline range and report styling
description:
Implement follow-up improvements for ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.

Behavior specs:

Feature: Score champion timeline display refinement

Scenario: Timeline has sparse champion activity inside a larger requested window
Given a report request covers a broad time window
And the earliest champion transition found is later than the requested start
When the ScoreChampionVersionTimeline output is generated
Then the effective chart range starts one day before the earliest in-window champion transition
And still ends at the requested end time.

Scenario: Timeline regions render with Plexus dashboard visual conventions
Given a ScoreChampionVersionTimeline report is rendered
When the user views score sections, SME information, metric summaries, tables, and diffs
Then regions are separated by flat theme background colors rather than thin borders or outlines
And primary report content uses the standard foreground text color so SME messages remain readable.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Started implementation. Scope is limited to ScoreChampionVersionTimeline backend effective date range normalization and dashboard renderer styling/contrast cleanup. Existing unrelated local changes will be left untouched.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented effective date range normalization and styling cleanup. Focused tests pass: backend ScoreChampionVersionTimeline tests now 10 passing; frontend renderer tests 6 passing. Live Suco 21-day run now reports requested_date_range 2026-04-11T18:07:40Z..2026-05-02T18:07:40Z and effective date_range 2026-04-28T19:40:06Z..2026-05-02T18:07:40Z, one day before first activity at 2026-04-29T19:40:06Z.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Verification complete: backend and frontend ScoreChampionVersionTimeline focused tests pass with effective range normalization and dashboard renderer styling updates.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Added include_unchanged option for ScoreChampionVersionTimeline. Default is changes-only: champion history entries without previousChampionVersionId are filtered out. CLI exposes --include-unchanged and Tactus passes include_unchanged through block_config/local runner. Focused tests pass. Live Suco 21-day default run now reports 35 scores, 48 champion changes, and 0 points without previous_champion_version_id.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Switched report diffs from raw preformatted unified diffs to Monaco DiffEditor using the existing project Monaco theme setup. Backend now includes raw left/right configuration and guidelines text alongside unified diff strings. Focused backend and frontend tests pass. Live Suco run confirms raw diff fields are present for real score changes; output saved to /tmp/suco_score_champion_version_timeline_cli_21d_monaco_diff.json.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Rendered SME agenda and worksheet content through ReactMarkdown with GFM and line-break support instead of preformatted text, preserving normal foreground contrast. Focused ScoreChampionVersionTimeline frontend test passes.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Adjusted the champion version table wrapper to explicitly size to row content: h-auto, max-h-none, self-start, and visible vertical overflow. Added focused renderer test coverage to prevent fixed-height table wrappers from returning. Test passed: ScoreChampionVersionTimeline frontend 7/7.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed visible Evaluation Record Cost from ScoreChampionVersionTimeline. The report now shows procedure cost only, both at the top-level summary and per-score metric cards. Updated docs language/sample accordingly. Focused frontend test passes 7/7.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Clarified champion change counting semantics. champion_change_count now counts only points with a previous champion version. New initial champions are counted separately as new_champion_count / scores_with_new_champions when include_unchanged is used. UI per-score label uses champion_change_count and appends new champion count only when present. Focused backend tests 12/12 and frontend tests 7/7 pass.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Aligned the ScoreChampionVersionTimeline chart with the optimizer/procedure metrics chart: removed the synthetic timeline_marker series that plotted dots at y=0, put dots on the actual feedback/regression metric series, normalized accuracy onto a 0-100 right axis, kept AC1 on the -1..1 left axis, and added procedure-style circle/square legend markers. Updated the focused frontend test to assert no timeline_marker line and correct axis assignment. Test: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Adjusted champion-transition markers so they no longer plot as metric values: markers now render on a separate bottom rail aligned to the shared x-domain, while AC1/accuracy dots remain only on real metric series. Focused frontend test still passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fixed likely report lockup source by lazy-mounting champion diffs: each score now shows a collapsed diff summary by default, and Monaco DiffEditor is only created after the user opens that score's diff. The tab content also mounts only the active diff editor instead of code and guidelines together. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed nested vertical scroll panes from the champion timeline report content: SME markdown now expands naturally instead of using max-height overflow, and diff fallback text no longer creates a vertical overflow pane. Remaining overflow in the block is horizontal-only for the version table. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Investigated why Address, Project Timeline, and Credit Rating Metadata AI appears. Fresh Suco output shows it is not a no-previous/no-evaluation row: it has previous_champion_version_id d3454470..., one optimizer procedure, feedback eval 73f78ae0... (AC1 0.8189, accuracy 85, 200 items), and regression eval c0b7197b... (AC1 0.7775, accuracy 85, 200 items). Also tightened backend inclusion so single-point champion rows with no evaluations/procedures are omitted. Backend test passes: pytest plexus/reports/blocks/score_champion_version_timeline_test.py -q.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reworked the champion version list so it no longer uses native table layout, which was preserving extra vertical space. It is now a compact ARIA table built from CSS grid rows, so a header plus one version row takes only header+row height. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Changed champion transition rail markers from dots to flat rounded pills and adjusted rail insets to align with the chart plot/x-axis scale. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fixed per-score optimization cost aggregation. Procedure costs now read both metadata.dashboard_state and metadata.state, and support the ProcedureTask cost shape (costs.evaluation.incurred_total/reused_total/total plus costs.inference.total). Procedure rows with no recorded cost now contribute 0 instead of making the score total N/A. Verified Suco 21-day fresh output: procedure scores now show dollar amounts/0.0 instead of N/A; total remains .4337581. Backend test passes: pytest plexus/reports/blocks/score_champion_version_timeline_test.py -q.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Updated SME rendering so the SME Information component is omitted entirely when there is no SME agenda. Removed the previous empty/no-agenda panel and added a frontend regression test. Focused test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fixed champion marker x-alignment by rendering the rounded pill markers inside the Recharts SVG via Customized, using the same x-axis scale as the chart ticks instead of a separate HTML rail with hand-computed percentages. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Tightened vertical spacing around the champion-version pill timeline by reducing chart bottom margin, lowering x-axis tick margin, and moving the SVG pill markers closer to the x-axis area. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reordered per-score sections so Champion Diff appears before SME markdown/long-form text. Diff remains collapsed/lazy-mounted by default. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Made SME agenda/worksheet markdown scannable by default: each block now shows a short clipped preview with Show more/Show less controls, preserving markdown rendering while avoiding long text forcing users to scroll past full SME content. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Moved champion-version pill markers below the chart plot area into the bottom margin so they no longer appear near the -1 AC1 region as data points. Horizontal x-axis alignment still comes from the Recharts x-scale. Focused frontend test passes: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Added score and score-version detail links to ScoreChampionVersionTimeline using the same lucide link icon/internal link styling pattern as evaluation task detail views. Focused frontend test now covers score, current version, previous champion, and latest champion links. Test run: npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx (pass).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Adjusted top ScoreChampionVersionTimeline summary metrics to remove the redundant Scores Analyzed tile and use a three-column desktop grid for the remaining six metrics.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Moved champion transition markers below the x-axis date labels by increasing the chart bottom margin and drawing the marker rectangles lower in the Recharts customized layer. This keeps them out of the AC1 plot area so they no longer read as -1 alignment values.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Replaced text expand buttons in ScoreChampionVersionTimeline with the standard report expand affordance from FeedbackAlignment/FeedbackContradictions: horizontal bg-border rule plus small ChevronDown/ChevronUp caret button. Applied to SME agenda/worksheet previews and champion diff expansion.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed horizontal scrolling from the champion-version table in ScoreChampionVersionTimeline. The table now uses full-width shrinkable grid columns instead of a min-width scroll container, and the focused test asserts no overflow-x-auto/min-w behavior.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Tightened the top padding on each score card in ScoreChampionVersionTimeline and moved the per-score title from text-base to the standard next size, text-lg.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Added per-score performance improvement summaries to ScoreChampionVersionTimeline. Backend now emits performance_summary comparing previous champion before the first in-window transition to the latest in-window champion. Frontend renders prominent side-by-side before/after Gauge cards for alignment and accuracy, with original/delta/new numbers, above the operational metric boxes. Tests: pytest plexus/reports/blocks/score_champion_version_timeline_test.py -q (15 passed); npm test -- --runTestsByPath components/blocks/__tests__/ScoreChampionVersionTimeline.test.tsx (10 passed).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Changed champion-transition marker rectangles in ScoreChampionVersionTimeline from foreground to muted-foreground so the x-axis champion markers are lower contrast.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Tightened vertical spacing between the champion-transition marker rail and chart legend by reducing ScoreChampionVersionTimeline chart bottom margin and legend top padding while keeping markers below x-axis labels.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Further tightened top padding on each ScoreChampionVersionTimeline score card from pt-3 to pt-2 so score titles sit closer to the top of the card.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Applied stronger visual tightening based on review feedback: score cards now have no top padding (pt-0), and champion-transition marker rectangles now use the muted background color hsl(var(--muted)) instead of muted-foreground.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Tightened the chart marker-to-legend spacing more aggressively: reduced chart bottom margin and pulled the legend upward with a negative top margin so it sits closer to the champion-transition marker rail.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed the remaining visual whitespace above score names by tightening the score title line height (leading-none) and applying a small standard negative top margin (-mt-1) to the score card header row. The card top padding was already pt-0; the remaining gap was the text line box.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Corrected champion-transition marker color to use Tailwind fill-muted directly with opacity-70, instead of relying on SVG fill attributes or muted-foreground. Test now asserts fill-muted and explicitly guards against foreground fill.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Changed champion-transition marker rectangles from fill-muted to fill-card per design review, keeping opacity-70 and existing shape/position.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Addressed repeated score-card header whitespace feedback at the header element level: score card remains pt-0, header row is pulled up from -mt-1 to -mt-3, and the score title explicitly sets m-0 plus leading-none to eliminate default heading/line-box whitespace above the title.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Adjusted ScoreChampionVersionTimeline rectangle corner radius to match the standard card radius. The score cards and rectangular report panels now use rounded-lg instead of rounded-md, while intentionally small controls/icons remain unchanged.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed the negative-margin workaround from the score card header and made the header flush with the score card top: the card remains pt-0, the h3 remains m-0/leading-none, and the header row now uses items-start instead of baseline alignment so the score title sits at the top edge without extra baseline offset.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Restructured each ScoreChampionVersionTimeline score card to make the score header a flush top block, separate from the padded body. The section has no top padding; the body below the header owns its own pt-3/pb-4 spacing. This removes top padding above the score name rather than relying on negative margin offsets.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fixed score card header alignment after review: header row now has a small pt-1 inset, score title is pulled up with -mt-1 to counter font metric slack, and the score ID/link gets pt-0.5 so it no longer touches the card top. This aligns the score title and score ID as one visual row.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed opacity from champion-transition marker rectangles. They now use exactly fill-card with no opacity modifier or SVG fill attribute.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Adjusted ScoreChampionVersionTimeline score card spacing to standard p-3 header padding. Removed the title/ID micro-offsets and matched the body to px-3 pb-3 so top and bottom card padding are balanced.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reworked score card header alignment: title and score ID are now in the same explicit title row with items-baseline, with the subtitle on its own row below. The card uses standard p-3 and the body starts with pt-3, avoiding the previous two-line flex/header mismatch.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed the h3 element for score card titles because global/user-agent heading styles were creating unwanted top margin in the rendered report. Score names now render as a div with role=heading aria-level=3 and controlled Tailwind line-height/spacing.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Added a small top inset to score cards now that title margin is controlled: score card spacing changed from p-3 to px-3 pb-3 pt-4 for slightly more top padding without reintroducing h3 margin behavior.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed the small standalone scorecard-name label above the score list in ScoreChampionVersionTimeline. Updated compacted-output test to assert that label is not rendered.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Moved the scorecard name into the ScoreChampionVersionTimeline report subtitle under the main block title. The old inline 'Scorecard: ...' label remains removed; tests now assert the subtitle displays the scorecard name.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Made the scorecard subtitle under ScoreChampionVersionTimeline a proper subhead instead of small muted text. ReportBlock now supports an optional subtitleClassName override, and this report passes text-lg font-semibold text-foreground for the scorecard name.
